approach.speaker: narrator
approach.text: He is at the corner table the inn keeps clear out of habit, hooded, hands wrapped around a cup that has not been refilled in a long while. He does not look up when you sit. He looks up only when the lamp catches a thing on you he was not expecting to see again.

quill-recognise.speaker: quill
quill-recognise.text: ...That. The mark on your hand. The shape under your collar. Where did you find that. Speak slowly. The answer matters more than you know.

quill-options.speaker: quill
quill-options.text: I will know if you lie. The mark only sits that way on a thing taken from the throne. Choose.

choices.text: I took it from the throne. I know what I wore.
What are you talking about? It is just an old keepsake.
[Stand up. Walk away from the table.]

knew.speaker: quill
knew.text: ...Aye. You knew. I can see it now in the way you do not flinch from the word. Sit, please. Sit a moment longer. I had thought I would not see that mark on a living person again. I had hoped not to.

knew-2.speaker: quill
knew-2.text: I cannot say all of it tonight. The hood is for a reason and the cup is for a reason. But you should come back. There is a thing I owe a stranger, and you appear to be the stranger. Find me at this corner. I will be here. I will always be here.

deny.speaker: quill
deny.text: ...Aye. A keepsake. Of course. Forgive an old drunk for staring. The lamp does strange things to a tired man's eye. Walk on. The corner is only a corner. Forget you saw me here.

leave.speaker: narrator
leave.text: You leave him with his cup. He does not call after you. When you reach the door, you glance back — he has not moved. The hood has not lifted. Whatever you wore on your collar, it has gone back to being, for him, a thing seen and not believed.
